title: Bridge Essay: Aberrant connectivity of areas for decoding degraded speech in patients with auditory verbal hallucinations.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.
**Phase context:** Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

**Aberrant connectivity of areas for decoding degraded speech in patients with auditory verbal hallucinations.** — Brain Struct Funct (2014). DOI: 10.1007/s00429-013-0519-5. PMID: 23423461. Auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH) are a hallmark of psychotic experience. Various mechanisms including misattribution of inner speech and imbalance between bottom-up and top-down factors in auditory perception potentially due to aberrant connectivity between frontal and temporo-parietal areas have been suggested to underlie AVH.
